message:
fix: detect and clean stale Dolt lock files (gt-dolt-lockfiles)

Fix bootstrap lock timeout that never fires: acquireBootstrapLock used
FlockExclusiveBlocking which blocks indefinitely, making the 30s timeout
unreachable. Switch to FlockExclusiveNonBlocking with polling so the
timeout actually works.

Add stale lock detection to bootstrap and sync operations:
- Bootstrap: remove lock files older than 5 minutes before acquiring
- Sync: detect and clean .sync.lock files older than 1 hour when
  TryLock fails, as a safety net for NFS/hung process edge cases

Add doctor check (CheckStaleLockFiles) that detects stale lock files:
- dolt.bootstrap.lock: age-based (>5 min threshold)
- .sync.lock: age-based (>1 hour threshold)
- daemon.lock: flock probe (file exists but no process holds lock)
- *.startlock: age-based (>30 sec threshold)

Wire into bd doctor --fix for automatic cleanup.

Export FlockExclusiveNonBlocking, ErrLocked, and IsLocked from the
lockfile package for use by bootstrap and other callers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: beads/polecats/quartz
Rig: beads
Role: polecats

files.patch:
@@ -183,6 +183,7 @@ func applyFixList(path string, fixes []doctorCheck) {
 	// Rough dependency chain:
 	// permissions/daemon cleanup → config sanity → DB integrity/migrations → DB↔JSONL sync.
 	order := []string{
+		"Lock Files",
 		"Permissions",
 		"Daemon Health",
 		"Database Config",
@@ -317,6 +318,8 @@ func applyFixList(path string, fixes []doctorCheck) {
 			err = doctor.FixStaleMQFiles(path)
 		case "Patrol Pollution":
 			err = fix.PatrolPollution(path)
+		case "Lock Files":
+			err = fix.StaleLockFiles(path)
 		default:
 			fmt.Printf("  ⚠ No automatic fix available for %s\n", check.Name)
 			fmt.Printf("  Manual fix: %s\n", check.Fix)
